description: Scaled benchmark program for OpenLocus retrieval quality across repository groups and task types. S/M/L/X tiers with increasing scale and label quality requirements. Current S/M data uses logical repo groups from one OpenLocus workspace snapshot; independent external repositories are a follow-up expansion. Safety foundation, not quality conclusion.

S.note: R14-S is populated and locally runnable over 4 logical repo groups from one OpenLocus workspace snapshot. 8 human_reviewed negative/stress labels + 37 mined_high_confidence symbol/impl labels + 3 mined config/policy labels.

M.note: M-tier uses the same 4 logical repo groups as S with additional mined tasks. Full M target requires 8+ independent repo groups/repositories. This is partial.

L.note: L-tier is placeholder with 10 weak-label tasks. Not populated: requires additional repos beyond current workspace.

X.note: X-tier not populated. Requires external repo sources. Running --tier X will fail.

generation_info.anti_leakage: public tasks contain no gold paths/lines; labels are private; runner copies declared sources into isolated roots and writes repo-lock policy.toml excludes fixtures/**/eval/**/docs/**/runs/**/.openlocus/**/target/**; runtime canary retrieval must return zero hits

metrics.notes: Span-level precision/recall metrics are computed but not yet meaningful due to coarse gold spans and small dataset size. File-level recall and MRR are the primary metrics for R14-S. citation_validity is a fail-closed safety gate (must be 1.0). negative_nonempty_rate@10 measures false positive rate on negative tasks.
